decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing the Doomsday combo to win the game by quickly assembling a winning combination of cards, such as Thassa's Oracle and Underground Sea. It includes various control elements and card draw spells to help find and protect the combo pieces. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional recursion and flexibility in the deck. The deck aims to efficiently and consistently execute its combo while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure victory in Vintage 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on generating card advantage and mana through efficient spells and artifacts to set up a powerful Paradoxical Outcome combo. By returning a large number of artifacts to hand with Paradoxical Outcome, the deck can generate more mana and draw more cards to find its win condition quickly. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds resilience and a reliable way to recur key artifacts if needed. The deck aims to control the game with countermagic and disruption while assembling its combo pieces for a game-winning turn.

This deck focuses on efficiently manipulating the top cards of the library to set up a game-winning Doomsday combo, either through Thassa's Oracle or a Lab Maniac win condition. The goal is to cast Doomsday to stack the top five cards of the library in a way that allows the player to draw into their win condition. The deck includes various control elements such as Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo and disruption spells like Duress and Misdirection to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on using artifact synergies to overwhelm the opponent with early aggression and disruption. By utilizing powerful artifacts like Black Lotus, Moxen, and Mishra's Workshop, along with hate cards like Null Rod and Sphere of Resistance, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while quickly deploying threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Stonecoil Serpent. Crucible of Worlds and Inventors' Fair provide additional utility and card advantage, creating a versatile and resourceful strategy that can adapt to various matchups in Vintage 1 vs 1.

This Dimir Control deck aims to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of countermagic, removal spells, and disruptive creatures like Psychic Frog. It also utilizes powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time to maintain card advantage. The deck's main win condition is to outvalue the opponent over time while maintaining control over the game with cards like Null Rod and Seal of Removal. With Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion, the deck gains additional value by recurring cheap permanents from the graveyard.

The deck focuses on quickly milling cards into the graveyard to enable powerful graveyard recursion and combo potential. Golgari Thug, Stinkweed Imp, and Golgari Grave-Troll help fill the graveyard, while cards like Narcomoeba and Prized Amalgam provide additional value when put into play from the graveyard. Hollow One and Shambling Shell can be cast for free from the graveyard, and cards like Creeping Chill provide additional reach. Force of Will and Force of Negation protect the combo, while Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder help dig for combo pieces.

The deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures while disrupting the opponent's game plan with hate cards like Archon of Emeria, Chancellor of the Annex, and Void Mirror. It aims to gain tempo advantage by using cards like Clarion Conqueror and Seasoned Dungeoneer to pressure the opponent while protecting its own creatures with Cavern of Souls and Karakas. The inclusion of cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Black Lotus allows for powerful plays and utility. This deck combines aggression with disruption to outpace and outwit the opponent in a fast-paced format like Vintage 1 vs 1.

The deck focuses on controlling the game using counterspells and disruption while also utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play. It includes a variety of powerful spells like Ancestral Recall and Demonic Tutor to manipulate the game state in your favor. The deck also has the ability to quickly search for key cards with tutors like Vampiric Tutor and Demonic Tutor. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while leveraging powerful creatures and spells to dominate the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control and disruption spells alongside efficient creatures to maintain board presence and disrupt opponent's game plan. It includes cards like Force of Will, Spell Pierce, and Collector Ouphe to counter opponent's spells, while also utilizing cards like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to deal with threats on the board. The deck also includes card drawing and searching spells like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Demonic Tutor to ensure it has the necessary resources to stay ahead in the game.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by disrupting opponent's strategies with counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, removal spells like Fatal Push, and disruptive artifacts like Null Rod. It also utilizes card advantage through spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time. The deck aims to protect its win conditions, such as Psychic Frog, with efficient removal and disruption spells while maintaining card advantage and board control to outvalue the opponent in a longer game.

The main idea of this deck is to efficiently ramp into powerful artifacts like Coveted Jewel using cards like Tolarian Academy and Mishra's Workshop, while also disrupting the opponent with counterspells like Force of Will. The deck also utilizes combo pieces like Time Vault and Manifold Key for extra turns, and has card advantage from Ancestral Recall and Vexing Bauble. Overall, the strategy revolves around assembling key pieces quickly and overwhelming the opponent with powerful artifacts and disruptive plays.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to utilize a combination of disruption spells and powerful creatures to control the game and outvalue the opponent. The deck runs a variety of counterspells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while also including card advantage spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time to stay ahead in resources. With the ability to generate card advantage, disrupt the opponent's plays, and establish a strong board presence, this deck aims to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory through control and resource management.

The deck aims to quickly ramp into powerful artifacts and combo pieces to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of aggro pressure and disruptive abilities. With a focus on efficient mana production and artifact synergy, the deck is designed to accelerate its game plan and push for a fast victory in a Vintage 1 vs 1 setting. The combination of threats and disruptive elements makes it a formidable force in the Aggro - MUD archetype, utilizing powerful artifacts like The One Ring and powerful mana acceleration like Mishra's Workshop to outpace and outmaneuver opponents.

The main goal of this deck is to assemble the Coveted Jewel combo, which involves using cards like Mishra's Workshop, Ancient Tomb, and Grim Monolith to generate mana quickly. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to protect the combo pieces. The "Displacer Kitten" card serves as a key enabler for the combo, allowing for extra card draw with Coveted Jewel. By using these cards in combination with powerful artifacts like The One Ring and mana acceleration, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents and win the game quickly.

The deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana quickly through cards like Mishra's Workshop, Grim Monolith, and Mana Crypt, while also utilizing cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Coveted Jewel to draw into key combo pieces. The goal is to set up a powerful board presence with artifacts like The One Ring and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, before ultimately winning the game with cards like Time Vault and Tinker. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Trinisphere to control the game and protect the combo.

The deck focuses on fast mana acceleration and powerful artifact synergy to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive effects. Key cards like The One Ring, Mishra's Workshop, and Nettlecyst enable explosive plays while cards like Manifold Key and Grim Monolith provide additional utility and versatility. The deck aims to control the board state with artifacts like Petrified Hamlet and City of Traitors, while also utilizing powerful win conditions like Karn, the Great Creator and Time Vault to close out games quickly.
